title: Horaninovia excellens
intro: Horaninovia excellens is a rare and endangered plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas, belonging to the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Horaninovia excellens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Horaninovia excellens is processed into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of various herbal capsules and tablets.

The classification of Horaninovia excellens places it within the order Brassicales. The exact classification hierarchy includes the kingdom Plantae, followed by the clade Angiosperms, and then the clade Eudicots. Further classification places it within the clade Rosids.
